channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: True crime reporters blocked outside courthouse where Karen Read is on trial file First Amendment lawsuit
item.description: Two true crime reporters have filed a lawsuit against Massachusetts State Police for allegedly blocking them outside the courthouse where Karen Read is on trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China hit brakes on TikTok deal after Trump announced wide-ranging tariffs, AP source says
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said he is signing an executive order to keep TikTok running in the U.S. for another 75 days to give his administration more time to broker a deal to bring the social media platform under American ownership.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, AAMER MADHANI and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Disgraced former Cardinal Theodore McCarrick has died at age 94
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Theodore McCarrick, a once-powerful Catholic cardinal who was defrocked by Pope Francis in 2019 after a Vatican investigation determined he had molested adults and children, has died. He was 94.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans are debating a higher tax rate for millionaires: Report
item.description: Republicans are reportedly considering raising taxes on millionaires to help offset the cost of their tax bill.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Divided Supreme Court sides with Trump to block teacher grants
item.description: A divided Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration by allowing officials to block $65 million in teacher development grants frozen over concerns they were promoting diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration issues demands on Harvard as conditions for billions in federal money
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has issued a list of demands Harvard University must meet as a condition for receiving almost $9 billion in grants and contracts, federal money that is being threatened during an investigation into campus antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas measles outbreak approaches 500 cases
item.description: The deadly measles outbreak in Texas is continuing to rise, now approaching a total of 500 confirmed cases with at least 56 people facing hospitalization since late January, according to health officials.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: 100-year-old tortoise gives birth to critically endangered hatchlings at Philly Zoo
item.description: For the first time in over 150 years, the Philadelphia Zoo has new additions to the Western Santa Cruz Galapagos tortoise family.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, Fox 5

item.title: US must return Maryland father mistakenly deported to El Salvador prison, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge on Friday ordered the Trump administration to arrange for the return of a Maryland man to the United States after he was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dow plunges 2,000 as sell-off for markets slams into a scarier gear following Trump's tariffs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The sell-off for financial markets worldwide is slamming into a higher, scarier gear on Friday. The S&P 500 tumbled 5.7% as Wall Street's worst crisis since the COVID crash deepened after China matched President Donald Trump’s big raise in tariffs announced earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: LA County reaches $4 billion agreement to settle sexual abuse claims at juvenile facilities
item.description: Officials say Los Angeles County has reached a $4 billion agreement to settle nearly 7,000 claims of sexual abuse in juvenile facilities since 1959.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dow plunges past 2,000 in a worsening global sell-off after China retaliates against Trump tariffs
item.description: The worldwide sell-off for financial markets is slamming into an even higher, scarier gear. The S&P 500 tumbled 5.7% Friday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average plunged 2,100 points and the Nasdaq composite dropped 5.7%.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Poor air quality increases depression risk
item.description: A new study finds poor air quality is linked to a heightened risk for depression. Depression is a classified as a mood disorder.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: TSA officers find prohibited item in traveler's flip-flop at DC airport
item.description: TSA officers working at Washington Dulles on March 30 were able to stop a traveler from bringing the item through security, a spokesperson for the agency said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: ICE arrests 37 in Washington roofing company raid
item.description: The company's CEO said those who were detained worked for the company for years as “tax-paying employees.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft's AI division head wants to create a lasting relationship between chatbots and their users
item.description: Fifty years after the founding of Microsoft, the CEO of its artificial intelligence division has a big task: develop a new product line as integral to daily life as the software giant's past innovations.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's tariffs hit uninhabited islands filled with penguins
item.description: The implementation of tariffs on the islands did not go unnoticed by Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Nearly half of National Weather Service offices have 20% vacancy rates, and experts say it's a risk
item.description: Data obtained by The Associated Press shows that close to half of the local National Weather Service forecast offices have 20% vacancy rates as severe weather chugs across the nation’s heartland.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tornadoes will become more frequent in coming weeks. Here's what to know if your home is destroyed
item.description: An average of 1,200 tornadoes hit the United States yearly, and the country will probably get more killer supercells spawning tornadoes and hail as the world warms, according to a 2023 study.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Karen Read appeals double jeopardy ruling to US Supreme Court
item.description: Karen Read's lawyers have taken her double jeopardy appeal to the United States Supreme Court with her second trial underway in the death of John O'Keefe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bondi announces charges in MS-13 killing as the Trump administration highlights its gang crackdown
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Three alleged MS-13 gang members have been federally charged in connection with a killing a decade ago in Florida, U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi announced Friday, seeking to highlight the Trump administration's push to prosecute violent gangs.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump calls for Marine Le Pen to be freed
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday defended embattled French far-right leader Marine Le Pen, arguing the “witch hunt” against her is yet another instance of critics in Europe using “lawfare” to quash free speech and censor political opposition.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Invited to the Met Gala, nothing to wear? Hint: Find yourself a 'superfine' suit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — What’s in a suit? According to curators busy prepping the newest Met Gala exhibit, a whole lot more than tailoring: history, culture, identity, power and, most of all, self-expression.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN NOVECK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senators introduce bipartisan bill to give Congress more power over tariffs
item.description: If passed, the Trade Review Act of 2025 would require the president to notify lawmakers of an imposition or increase in tariffs within 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Federal officials are quietly terminating the legal residency of some international college students
item.description: A crackdown on foreign students is alarming college leaders, who say the Trump administration is using new tactics and vague justifications to push some students out of the country.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Annie Ma And Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: In the latest poll, 37 percent of respondents approved of Trump’s handling of the economy and 30 percent approved of his approach to addressing the high cost of living in the country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Ford offering employee pricing to all shoppers in wake of Trump tariffs
item.description: The campaign comes as Trump has rolled out a fresh package of tariffs, impacting nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: K-12 schools must sign certification against DEI to receive federal money, administration says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As a condition for receiving federal money, the Trump administration is ordering K-12 schools to certify that they are following federal civil rights laws and ending any discriminatory diversity, equity and inclusion practices.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Transgender student's arrest for violating Florida bathroom law is thought to be a first
item.description: A transgender college student declared “I am here to break the law” before entering a women’s restroom at the Florida State Capitol and being led out in handcuffs by police.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Natchitoches, Louisiana student wins first place in national competition
item.description: Brandon Jordan, a student from Northwestern State University, had to troubleshoot a virtual computer for the competition.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Woods, NewsNation

item.title: Remains of Illinois woman who disappeared in 1983 found after cold case investigators start podcast
item.description: Karen Schepers' remains were finally identified after being found in a car submerged in the Fox River, nearly 43 years after she went missing, thanks to the efforts of the Elgin Police Department and the non-profit organization Chaos Divers.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Oz as head of CMS along party lines
item.description: The Senate has confirmed Mehmet Oz as administrator for the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS), making him the head of Medicaid at a time where its funding is at risk of being severely cut. The Senate voted 53-45 along party lines to confirm Oz.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Dow drops 1,600 as US stocks lead worldwide sell-off after Trump’s tariffs cause a COVID-like shock
item.description: Financial markets around the world are reeling Thursday following President Donald Trump’s latest and most severe set of tariffs, and the U.S. stock market is taking the worst of it so far.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Yale fires legal scholar amid review of possible ties to 'sham charity' for designated terror group
item.description: Yale Law School has fired an Iranian scholar, accusing her of refusing to cooperate as it probed allegations that she is involved with a group that the U.S. calls a “sham charity” for a designated terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dow drops 1,600 as US stocks lead worldwide sell-off after Trump’s tariffs cause a COVID-like shock
item.description: Financial markets around the world are reeling Thursday following President Donald Trump’s latest and most severe set of tariffs, and the U.S. stock market is taking the worst of it so far.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 launches in June with new 'Mario Kart World' game
item.description: Nintendo has announced a June 5 launch date for its latest gaming console, the Switch 2, which will introduce interactive chat and screenshare functions to connect gamers.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
